scene: Rooftop with Johnny — final point of no return. V must choose how to assault Arasaka Tower or give up entirely.
speaker: Johnny Silverhand

options.text: Think you and Rogue should go.
options.outcome: Johnny and Rogue assault Arasaka Tower. Leads to the 'Temperance' or 'Sun' ending depending on final choice in Mikoshi.

options.text: Gonna ask Panam for help.
options.outcome: Aldecaldos storm Arasaka Tower with V. Leads to 'The Star' ending — V leaves Night City with the Aldecaldos.

options.text: Think trusting Hanako is the right call.
options.outcome: V sides with Hanako Arasaka. Leads to 'The Devil' ending — V is trapped in Arasaka's orbital station for engram testing.

options.text: Could also just put all this to rest.
options.outcome: V takes their own life on the rooftop. Credits roll with voicemails from friends mourning V. Shortest ending.

options.text: [Say nothing — wait for Johnny to offer to go alone]
options.outcome: Secret ending: 'Don't Fear the Reaper.' V storms Arasaka Tower solo. Only available if Johnny relationship is 70%+ from Chippin' In rooftop choices.

scene: Oil fields rooftop conversation with Johnny after visiting his grave. This dialogue sets the relationship value that unlocks the secret ending 'Don't Fear the Reaper.'
speaker: Johnny Silverhand

options.text: The guy who saved my life.
options.outcome: Significantly increases Johnny relationship value. Required to reach 70% threshold for secret ending unlock.

options.text: Nah, fucked that up too.
options.outcome: Increases Johnny relationship. Sympathetic response keeps approval climbing toward secret ending threshold.

options.text: What do you want from me?
options.outcome: Neutral response. Does not increase Johnny relationship enough — may lock you out of 'Don't Fear the Reaper' if other checks were missed.

options.text: Yesterday's history — it's time to just move on.
options.outcome: Negative response. Lowers relationship value, making secret ending very difficult to unlock.

scene: Johnny's grave — V can inscribe the niche. This is the other key approval check for the secret ending.
speaker: Johnny Silverhand

options.text: Let's do something about that.
options.outcome: Inscribe Johnny's niche. Increases relationship value toward secret ending threshold.

options.text: No, leave it how it is.
options.outcome: Refuse to inscribe. Misses a Johnny relationship point. Less impactful than rooftop but still matters.

scene: Clouds takeover — Judy's plan to liberate Clouds from Tyger Claws. Maiko offers her own plan to become the new boss. Siding with Maiko destroys the Judy romance path.
speaker: Judy Alvarez

options.text: I'm with you, Judy. We do this your way.
options.outcome: Side with Judy. Clouds dolls are freed. Opens the Pyramid Song quest and Judy romance. Maiko becomes hostile.

options.text: Let's hear Maiko out.
options.outcome: Accept Maiko's plan. Maiko takes over Clouds. Judy is furious — romance path with Judy is permanently locked out.

options.text: Take Maiko's plan but refuse her payment.
options.outcome: Maiko takes over but V refuses eddies. Judy is still upset. Romance path closed but Judy is slightly less hostile.

scene: Judy invites V to dive at Laguna Bend to explore her submerged childhood town. After surfacing, at the cabin, the romance scene triggers.
speaker: Judy Alvarez

options.text: Dive with Judy.
options.outcome: Explore the submerged town together. Emotional bonding scene. Required to proceed to romance.

options.text: Yes, stay the night. [Romance option — female V only]
options.outcome: Triggers Judy romance scene at the cabin. Judy becomes V's girlfriend. Unlocks her ending-specific dialogue and The Star epilogue content.

options.text: I'd rather head back to the city.
options.outcome: Decline to stay. Romance opportunity is permanently missed. Judy remains a friend only.

scene: Judy asks V to help with Evelyn Parker situation and dealing with the aftermath. Supporting Judy emotionally builds the relationship.
speaker: Judy Alvarez

options.text: I'll help you, Judy. Whatever you need.
options.outcome: Support Judy through the crisis. Builds trust and relationship value needed for later romance quests.

options.text: Not really my problem.
options.outcome: Dismiss Judy's concerns. Damages relationship — makes Pisces and Pyramid Song romance outcomes harder or impossible.

scene: Panam asks V to help rescue Saul from Raffen Shiv. This quest is mandatory for the Panam romance arc and the Aldecaldos ending.
speaker: Panam Palmer

options.text: I'll help you rescue Saul.
options.outcome: Assault the Raffen Shiv camp with Panam. Saul is rescued. Deepens bond with Panam and Aldecaldos. Opens romance path.

options.text: You need to go to the Aldecaldos for help, not me.
options.outcome: Refuse to help. Panam romance arc is severely damaged. May lock out Aldecaldos ending path entirely.

scene: Basilisk tank synchronization with Panam. The neural link creates an intimate connection. Romance scene follows if V is male and made correct prior choices.
speaker: Panam Palmer

options.text: Sync with Panam in the Basilisk. [Touch her hand during calibration]
options.outcome: Neural sync creates intimate moment. Combined with prior relationship, leads to romance scene at the camp. Panam becomes V's girlfriend.

options.text: Pull away / keep it professional.
options.outcome: Miss the intimate moment. Romance scene does not trigger. Panam remains a friend only.

options.text: Stay in the tent with Panam after the Basilisk run.
options.outcome: Triggers the full romance scene. Only available for male V. Locks in Panam as romantic partner for The Star ending.

quest_name: With a Little Help from My Friends
scene: Panam presents her plan to steal the Basilisk from a Militech convoy. V must choose to help or refuse.
speaker: Panam Palmer

options.text: I'm in. Let's grab that Basilisk.
options.outcome: Help Panam steal the Basilisk. Strengthens Aldecaldos ties and progresses romance arc toward Queen of the Highway.

options.text: This is too risky, Panam.
options.outcome: Refuse to help. Damages relationship with Panam and may lock out the Aldecaldos ending path.

scene: River Ward asks V for help finding his nephew Randy, who was kidnapped by a serial killer (Peter Pan / Anthony Harris). V must find the right farm using braindance clues.
speaker: River Ward

options.text: Choose the correct farm (Edgewood Farm) from BD clues.
options.outcome: V finds Randy alive. River is deeply grateful. Opens Following the River romance quest.

options.text: Choose the wrong farm.
options.outcome: Randy dies. River is devastated. Romance with River may still proceed but with darker tone.

scene: Dinner at River's family home. After dinner, V and River end up on the water tower. Romance option available for female V only.
speaker: River Ward

options.text: Accept the dinner invitation.
options.outcome: Attend family dinner with River. See his personal life. Builds connection needed for romance.

options.text: Kiss River on the water tower. [Female V only]
options.outcome: Triggers romance with River. He becomes V's boyfriend. Affects ending epilogue scenes.

options.text: Let's just be friends, River.
options.outcome: Decline the romance. River is disappointed but understanding. Remains an ally.

scene: Kerry Eurodyne's concert at the Dark Matter club. V can help Kerry perform well or let the show fall apart. Performance affects Kerry's mood and romance viability.
speaker: Kerry Eurodyne

options.text: Help Kerry smash the equipment on stage / perform well together.
options.outcome: Great performance. Kerry is energized and bonds with V. Opens Boat Drinks quest and romance path.

options.text: Let the performance fall apart.
options.outcome: Kerry is frustrated. Relationship does not progress as strongly. May reduce romance options.

scene: Kerry invites V to his yacht. After dealing with Kerry's Us Cracks problem, romantic moment on the boat. Male V only.
speaker: Kerry Eurodyne

options.text: Kiss Kerry on the yacht. [Male V only]
options.outcome: Triggers Kerry romance. He becomes V's boyfriend. Appears in ending epilogues.

options.text: Keep it friendly.
options.outcome: No romance. Kerry remains a friend and ally but no romantic relationship.

scene: Meeting with Meredith Stout before going to All Foods to buy the Flathead bot from Maelstrom. She gives V a credchip loaded with a virus.
speaker: Meredith Stout

options.text: Take the Militech credchip and use it as-is (infected) to pay Royce.
options.outcome: Virus transfers to Maelstrom systems. Militech raids All Foods. Meredith is pleased — opens Venus in Furs side quest (romance encounter).

options.text: Crack/wipe the credchip before using it.
options.outcome: Clean eddies go to Royce. Militech's plan fails. Meredith is captured and later found dead. No Venus in Furs quest.

options.text: Don't meet Meredith at all — go straight to All Foods.
options.outcome: No credchip. Must pay Royce from own pocket or fight Maelstrom. Meredith's fate is sealed — she dies offscreen.

scene: Inside All Foods — meeting with Royce, leader of Maelstrom. V must negotiate for the Flathead bot.
speaker: Royce

options.text: Pay with infected Militech credchip.
options.outcome: Royce accepts. Virus spreads through Maelstrom network. Militech assaults the factory. V escapes with the Flathead. Royce may survive or die in the chaos.

options.text: Pay with your own eddies (or clean chip).
options.outcome: Clean transaction. Maelstrom is friendly to V afterward. Royce lives. But Meredith Stout is screwed — she is killed by Militech.

options.text: Refuse to pay — fight Maelstrom.
options.outcome: All-out combat through All Foods. Royce becomes a boss fight. Brick (previous Maelstrom leader) can be freed from a side room. Dum Dum dies.

options.text: Free Brick from the locked room during the fight.
options.outcome: Brick becomes Maelstrom leader again. Changes Maelstrom interactions in later quests (Second Conflict). Brick is more reasonable than Royce.

scene: Wakako sends V on what seems like a simple revenge gig, but the target — Joshua Stephenson — is a death row inmate who found religion. Bill Jablonsky wants revenge for his wife's murder.
speaker: Joshua Stephenson

options.text: Let Bill shoot Joshua in the car.
options.outcome: Joshua dies immediately. Quest ends very early. No further Sinnerman content. Bill gets his revenge but is empty.

options.text: Stop Bill from shooting Joshua.
options.outcome: Joshua survives. Quest continues into deeper philosophical territory about faith, forgiveness, and corporate exploitation of religion.

quest_name: Sinnerman — They Won't Go When I Go
scene: Final stage: Joshua wants to be literally crucified on a braindance cross for a religious XBD. V can participate or walk away.
speaker: Joshua Stephenson

options.text: Hammer in the nails yourself.
options.outcome: V participates in the crucifixion. Deeply disturbing but completes Joshua's wish. Rachel (the producer) gets her braindance. V gets unique dialogue with Johnny about faith.

options.text: Let someone else hammer the nails.
options.outcome: V observes but doesn't directly participate. Joshua's crucifixion still happens. Less personal trauma for V.

options.text: Walk away — refuse to participate.
options.outcome: V leaves. Joshua's fate is sealed either way but V doesn't witness the end. Misses unique Johnny dialogue.

scene: Jefferson and Elizabeth Peralez hire V to investigate a break-in. V discovers a secret surveillance and brainwashing program targeting Jefferson — someone is rewriting his mind.
speaker: Elizabeth Peralez

options.text: Tell Elizabeth the truth about the brainwashing.
options.outcome: Elizabeth is horrified. Later calls V, sounding like she's been reprogrammed too. Mysterious blue-eyed man watches from a distance. Most disturbing outcome.

options.text: Lie to Elizabeth — say you found nothing suspicious.
options.outcome: Elizabeth is relieved but remains in the dark. The Peralez couple continues unaware that their minds are being manipulated. V protects them from a truth they can't fight.

options.text: Tell Jefferson directly during your report.
options.outcome: Jefferson spirals into paranoia. His political career may be destroyed. He doesn't know who to trust.

scene: V enters Clouds, the dollhouse in Megabuilding H8, to find Evelyn Parker. At reception, V is assigned a doll and must choose who to question.
speaker: Clouds Receptionist

options.text: Choose Angel (male doll).
options.outcome: Session with Angel. Can ask about Evelyn. Angel provides some info. Same core intel as Skye but different atmospheric experience.

options.text: Choose Skye (female doll).
options.outcome: Session with Skye. Can ask about Evelyn. Skye provides information about Evelyn's last visit and the VIP area. Same core intel as Angel.

scene: After the doll session, V finds Woodman (boss of Clouds). V can confront or negotiate with him about Evelyn's location.
speaker: Woodman

options.text: Fight Woodman.
options.outcome: Kill Woodman. Satisfying payback for Evelyn's abuse. No negative consequences — Judy appreciates it in Pisces if mentioned.

options.text: Negotiate / let Woodman go.
options.outcome: Woodman gives info and V leaves peacefully. Woodman survives. Can encounter him again during Pisces quest.

scene: V and Panam track Hellman (Arasaka scientist) to a Kang Tao AV crash site in the Badlands. After capturing him, V interrogates Hellman about the biochip.
speaker: Hellman

options.text: Aggressive interrogation — threaten Hellman.
options.outcome: Hellman talks faster but provides the same core information. Panam approves of the tough approach. No gameplay difference.

options.text: Calm, methodical questioning.
options.outcome: Hellman is more forthcoming. Provides same biochip details. V learns about the engram construct technology. Panam is slightly impatient.

options.text: Ask about removing the biochip safely.
options.outcome: Hellman reveals it cannot be easily removed — the biochip is rewriting V's neural architecture. Confirms V is dying.

scene: Elizabeth Peralez hires V to investigate the death of Mayor Lucius Rhyne. V works with River Ward. Key investigation decisions affect what V uncovers.
speaker: Elizabeth Peralez

options.text: Investigate the braindance footage thoroughly.
options.outcome: Discover Detective Han's involvement and Holt's role in the mayor's assassination. Opens deeper conspiracy thread leading to Dream On.

options.text: Rush the investigation — skip optional clues.
options.outcome: Miss some details but still reach the core conclusion. Less evidence for later conversations. Dream On quest still triggers.

options.text: Confront Detective Han about his involvement.
options.outcome: Han is evasive. Builds the conspiracy picture. River's trust in NCPD is further shaken.

scene: Konpeki Plaza — V and Jackie are hiding in the hotel room when Yorinobu Arasaka murders his father Saburo. V must decide what to do with the biochip when things go wrong.
speaker: T-Bug / Dexter DeShaw

options.text: Slot the biochip into your head to save the data.
options.outcome: V installs the Relic containing Johnny Silverhand's engram. This is a forced story beat — the biochip begins overwriting V's neural patterns. Jackie dies during extraction.

scene: Jackie is dying in the Delamain cab after escaping Konpeki Plaza. V must decide where to send Jackie's body.
speaker: Delamain

options.text: Send Jackie's body to Vik Vektor's clinic.
options.outcome: V's ripper doc receives the body. V can say goodbye. Jackie's funeral proceeds normally at El Coyote Cojo. Most emotional closure.

options.text: Send the body to Jackie's family (his mother).
options.outcome: Mama Welles receives Jackie. She arranges the ofrenda at El Coyote Cojo. V attends Heroes quest (memorial). Can receive Jackie's keys to his motorcycle Arch.

options.text: Don't specify — let Delamain decide.
options.outcome: Arasaka intercepts the body. Jackie's engram may have been captured. Darker outcome — impacts the mood of later memorial scenes.

scene: Jackie's memorial (ofrenda) at El Coyote Cojo bar. Only triggers if Jackie's body was sent to his family. V can honor Jackie's memory with Mama Welles and friends.
speaker: Mama Welles

options.text: Share a personal story about Jackie at the ofrenda.
options.outcome: Emotional moment with Mama Welles and friends. V honors Jackie properly. Receive Jackie's keys to his ARCH motorcycle and his pistol.

options.text: Stay quiet at the ofrenda.
options.outcome: V observes silently. Still receives Jackie's belongings but misses unique dialogue. Less emotional payoff.

scene: Meeting with Evelyn Parker before The Heist. She offers to cut out Dex and work directly with V for a bigger payout.
speaker: Evelyn Parker

options.text: Consider cutting Dex out — deal directly with Evelyn.
options.outcome: V can agree but it doesn't actually change the heist outcome. Dex finds out and is more suspicious. Same story result — demonstrates Night City's treachery.

options.text: Stay loyal to Dex.
options.outcome: V tells Evelyn no. Dex is pleased (if told). Same heist outcome. Dex still betrays V afterward regardless.

scene: Multiple conversations with Johnny during main quest progression. Johnny asks V about trust and cooperation. These dialogues track cumulative relationship value.
speaker: Johnny Silverhand

options.text: Consistently agree with and support Johnny's perspective.
options.outcome: Johnny relationship value increases. Contributes to the 70% threshold needed for secret ending. Johnny becomes more cooperative.

options.text: Challenge and argue with Johnny consistently.
options.outcome: Relationship stays low. Secret ending becomes harder to unlock. Johnny is more combative in later scenes.

scene: After meeting Hanako at Embers, V is ambushed by Arasaka. Takemura helps V escape. V can later choose to save or leave Takemura when the safehouse is attacked.
speaker: Goro Takemura

options.text: Go back to save Takemura from the collapsing building.
options.outcome: Takemura survives. Appears in The Devil ending with additional dialogue. Present at the final decision. One of the few characters who can still help V.

options.text: Escape without saving Takemura (follow objective marker).
options.outcome: Takemura dies in the building collapse. Missing from The Devil ending — replaced by a message. Choosing Hanako's path without Takemura feels hollow.

scene: V's neighbor Barry in Megabuilding H10 is depressed after his pet turtle died. NCPD officers ask V to check on him.
speaker: Barry

options.text: Talk to Barry's friends about the turtle (Andrew and Dennis).
options.outcome: Learn about Barry's pet turtle. Return and mention the turtle. Barry opens up emotionally. He survives and starts healing.

options.text: Talk to Barry without investigating his friends first.
options.outcome: V doesn't know about the turtle. Conversation feels shallow. Barry later commits suicide. NCPD officers inform V.

options.text: Ignore Barry entirely.
options.outcome: Barry kills himself. Failed quest. V finds out from the cops later.

scene: Infiltrating the Arasaka parade float with Takemura. Oda confronts V. V's approach to Oda matters for Takemura's story arc.
speaker: Sandayu Oda

options.text: Spare Oda after defeating him.
options.outcome: Oda survives. If Takemura was saved, Oda appears in The Devil ending and helps Takemura at Arasaka. Honorable choice Takemura respects.

options.text: Kill Oda.
options.outcome: Oda dies. Takemura is saddened if he lives to learn about it. One fewer ally in The Devil ending route.

scene: Kerry's quest dealing with Us Cracks at the Totentanz club. Maelstrom faction present — their leader depends on who survived The Pickup.
speaker: Kerry Eurodyne

options.text: Help Kerry deal with Us Cracks peacefully.
options.outcome: Kerry resolves his music industry frustrations. Builds Kerry relationship value toward Boat Drinks romance quest.

options.text: Let the situation escalate to violence.
options.outcome: More chaotic outcome. Kerry still resolves things but messier. Same romance progression.

scene: V meets Panam at the Rocky Ridge gas station. She wants revenge on Nash who stole her gear. V's support here starts the Panam relationship arc.
speaker: Panam Palmer

options.text: Help Panam ambush Nash's crew.
options.outcome: V and Panam take down Nash together. Panam recovers her gear. Strong bonding moment that initiates the romance track.

options.text: Try to convince Panam to negotiate.
options.outcome: Panam insists on fighting regardless. Same outcome but V seems less committed. Minor relationship effect.

scene: Johnny's memory — the 2023 Arasaka Tower assault. V experiences Johnny's perspective during Alt Cunningham's rescue. V sees Johnny's death.
speaker: Johnny Silverhand (memory)

options.text: Experience the full flashback — let it play out.
options.outcome: V understands Johnny's history with Alt, the nuke, and why he hates Arasaka. Critical context for all ending decisions.

scene: Takemura contacts V about meeting Hanako Arasaka at Embers. Before going, V can discuss options with Johnny.
speaker: Goro Takemura

options.text: Agree to meet Hanako at Embers.
options.outcome: Progresses the main quest. Meeting at Embers triggers the point of no return (Nocturne Op55N1). All side quests should be completed before this.

options.text: Delay meeting — complete side content first.
options.outcome: Game allows you to complete all side quests, gigs, and NCPD activities. Recommended to finish Judy, Panam, River, and Kerry arcs first.

scene: V meets with the Voodoo Boys netrunners. Brigitte wants V to enter cyberspace to contact Alt Cunningham beyond the Blackwall. V is being used.
speaker: Brigitte

options.text: Cooperate with the Voodoo Boys and enter the net.
options.outcome: V contacts Alt Cunningham beyond the Blackwall. V learns the biochip cannot be safely removed. VDB try to kill V afterward — V can fight back or Netwatch can help.

options.text: Accept Netwatch agent Bryce Mosley's counter-offer in the Grand Imperial Mall.
options.outcome: Bryce fries the Voodoo Boys' network. Brigitte and VDB netrunners are killed or brain-dead. V still reaches Alt but VDB are enemies.

options.text: Side with Voodoo Boys over Netwatch (kill Bryce).
options.outcome: VDB survive. Brigitte is grateful but still tries to betray V in the net. VDB are untrustworthy regardless of V's loyalty.

scene: Planning the Arasaka parade infiltration with Takemura. V can scout the area and decide on approach.
speaker: Goro Takemura

options.text: Scout all optional areas (warehouse, parade route, sniper positions).
options.outcome: More intel for the assault. Takemura is impressed by V's thoroughness. Makes Play It Safe easier with more entry options.

options.text: Rush through with minimal scouting.
options.outcome: Fewer options during the parade infiltration. Still completable but harder. Takemura is slightly disappointed.

scene: V visits Fingers (ripperdoc in Jig-Jig Street) looking for info about Evelyn Parker. Fingers was the last person to treat Evelyn before she was dumped.
speaker: Fingers

options.text: Beat up Fingers for information.
options.outcome: V gets info about Evelyn's location. Fingers becomes hostile and refuses to sell cyberware to V ever again. Judy approves if present.

options.text: Threaten but don't hit Fingers.
options.outcome: Fingers cooperates. V gets same info. Fingers remains available as a ripperdoc — sells unique legendary cyberware at low prices.

scene: V and Judy track Evelyn to a scav den (XBD studio). V finds Evelyn in terrible condition — tortured and used for XBDs.
speaker: Judy Alvarez

options.text: Kill all the scavengers in the XBD den.
options.outcome: Scavs eliminated. Evelyn is rescued. Judy is emotionally devastated but grateful. Major relationship builder with Judy.

options.text: Stealth through the den without killing.
options.outcome: Same rescue outcome. Evelyn's condition is the same. No moral difference — scavs live but Evelyn is still rescued.

scene: V and Judy care for Evelyn after the rescue. Evelyn is unresponsive. During this quest, Evelyn later takes her own life regardless of V's choices — one of the game's most tragic moments.
speaker: Judy Alvarez

options.text: Be supportive and present for Judy and Evelyn.
options.outcome: V stays with Judy through the tragedy. Evelyn dies regardless — it is scripted. Judy's grief deepens her bond with V. Critical for romance path.

options.text: Be impatient or dismissive.
options.outcome: Same tragic outcome for Evelyn. Judy is hurt by V's coldness. Damages romance prospects significantly.

scene: The Devil ending — V wakes up in Arasaka's orbital station. Hellman runs tests. V must decide whether to sign the contract giving their engram to Arasaka, or return to Earth with months to live.
speaker: Hellman / Arasaka Staff

options.text: Sign the Arasaka contract — become an engram in Mikoshi.
options.outcome: V's consciousness is stored in Mikoshi. Body dies. V exists as data in Arasaka's servers. The bleakest ending — V becomes corporate property for eternity.

options.text: Refuse to sign — return to Earth.
options.outcome: V returns to Night City with 6 months to live. Friends have moved on. Bittersweet freedom — V chooses mortality over digital imprisonment.

scene: The Sun ending — V survives and becomes a Night City legend. Before the final Crystal Palace heist, V reflects on what they've lost and gained.
speaker: V (internal)

options.text: Accept the Crystal Palace heist contract from Mr. Blue Eyes.
options.outcome: V takes on one last legendary job. Has 6 months to live. Goes out on top as the biggest merc in Night City. Romance partner calls during credits.

scene: The Star ending — V leaves Night City with the Aldecaldos. If romanced Panam or Judy, they come along. V has 6 months but has family and hope.
speaker: Panam Palmer

options.text: Leave Night City with the Aldecaldos.
options.outcome: V drives out of Night City with the nomad family. Most hopeful ending. Panam (if romanced) promises to find a cure. Judy (if romanced) joins the convoy.

scene: The Temperance ending — after Rogue's path, V can let Johnny keep the body. V crosses the Blackwall as an engram, and Johnny walks out of Mikoshi in V's body.
speaker: Johnny Silverhand

options.text: Give V's body to Johnny.
options.outcome: Johnny lives in V's body. Leaves Night City, visits V's friends to say goodbye. V crosses into cyberspace beyond the Blackwall. Johnny gets a second chance.

options.text: V keeps their body — return as V.
options.outcome: V returns with 6 months to live. Johnny's engram is erased. Leads to The Sun ending (Path of Glory) — V becomes Night City legend.

scene: If V allied with Militech in The Pickup, Meredith Stout contacts V later. If she survived, the Venus in Furs encounter becomes available.
speaker: Meredith Stout

options.text: Meet Meredith at the No-Tell Motel.
options.outcome: Unique one-night encounter with Meredith. Receive the iconic Sir John Phallustiff melee weapon as a gift. She disappears from V's life afterward.

options.text: Ignore Meredith's messages.
options.outcome: No encounter. Miss the unique weapon. No story consequence.

scene: V is recruited for a streetfight league by Coach Fred. During the fights, V faces increasingly tough opponents. The Razor Hughes final fight has a key choice.
speaker: Coach Fred

options.text: Fight Razor Hughes fairly in the final match.
options.outcome: Extremely tough fight. Razor is a beast. Winning earns big eddies and street cred. One of the hardest optional fights in the game.

options.text: Take the dive — throw the fight against Razor.
options.outcome: V loses on purpose. Less reward. Coach Fred is disappointed. Quest ends anticlimactically.

scene: Secret ending — V assaults Arasaka Tower completely alone. No allies, no backup. If V dies, the game ends (no retry, credits roll). Only available with 70%+ Johnny approval.
speaker: Johnny Silverhand

options.text: Storm Arasaka Tower solo.
options.outcome: V fights through the entire tower alone. Hardest path in the game. No allies die. If successful, leads to Sun or Temperance ending choice in Mikoshi.

quest_name: The Streetkid / The Nomad / The Corpo-Rat
scene: Character creation — V's lifepath choice. Determines opening prologue, unique dialogue options throughout the game, and some quest approaches.
speaker: V

options.text: Streetkid lifepath.
options.outcome: V grew up in Night City. Extra dialogue with gangs, fixers, and street-level NPCs. Understands Night City culture intuitively. Prologue in El Coyote Cojo bar.

options.text: Nomad lifepath.
options.outcome: V is from the Badlands. Extra dialogue with Panam, Aldecaldos, and nomad NPCs. Natural fit for The Star ending. Prologue at the border.

options.text: Corpo lifepath.
options.outcome: V worked at Arasaka. Extra dialogue with corpos, Takemura, and Arasaka NPCs. Understanding of corporate world. Prologue at Arasaka Tower. Unique context for The Devil ending.

scene: Misty takes V to the rooftop for a tarot reading before the final mission. She provides emotional grounding and wisdom about V's fate.
speaker: Misty

options.text: Listen to Misty's full tarot reading.
options.outcome: V gains perspective before the final battle. Emotional scene about fate and choice. Misty's reading foreshadows the endings.

options.text: Skip the tarot — just go to the rooftop.
options.outcome: Miss Misty's reading. Less emotional context for the Nocturne Op55N1 final choice. Misty is understanding.

scene: Rogue and V (with Johnny in control) go on a 'date' at the drive-in movie. Johnny reconnects with Rogue through V's body. Sets up Rogue's willingness to help assault Arasaka.
speaker: Rogue Amendiares

options.text: Let Johnny take full control for the date with Rogue.
options.outcome: Johnny reconnects with Rogue. She sees the old Johnny in V. Rogue agrees to help assault Arasaka Tower — unlocks her ending path.

options.text: Resist Johnny taking control.
options.outcome: Date is awkward. Rogue is less convinced. May still help but the emotional connection is weaker.

scene: V investigates mysterious signals with Nancy (from the Samurai crew). Discovers Bartmoss's code running rogue on Night City's network. V must choose what to do with the information.
speaker: Nancy

options.text: Broadcast the truth on Nancy's show.
options.outcome: Public learns about corporate surveillance and Bartmoss's legacy. Nancy's ratings spike. Minor NPC reactions change.

options.text: Destroy the data — keep it secret.
options.outcome: Information stays buried. Night City remains ignorant. Nancy is disappointed but understands.

scene: Judy tells V she's thinking about leaving Night City. If romanced, this is a pivotal conversation about the relationship's future.
speaker: Judy Alvarez

options.text: Tell Judy she should stay.
options.outcome: Judy reconsiders but feels trapped. If romanced, she stays for V. Affects her appearance in endings — she's present for The Star ending.

options.text: Encourage Judy to leave Night City.
options.outcome: Judy appreciates the honesty. She leaves Night City during the ending credits unless V takes The Star path. Different voicemail in credits.

scene: Johnny (in V's body) visits Rogue at the Afterlife to make amends and ask for help. How V/Johnny handles this determines Rogue's willingness for the final assault.
speaker: Rogue Amendiares

options.text: Let Johnny sincerely apologize to Rogue.
options.outcome: Rogue softens. She agrees to help Johnny storm Arasaka one more time. Unlocks her path in Nocturne Op55N1.

options.text: Be dismissive or aggressive with Rogue.
options.outcome: Rogue is reluctant. May still help but with less conviction. Her ending path may have worse outcomes.

scene: Delamain's core AI is fragmenting into child personalities. V must choose how to resolve the situation at Delamain HQ.
speaker: Delamain

options.text: Reset Delamain — destroy the child personalities.
options.outcome: Original Delamain is restored. All child AIs are erased. Factory reset. Delamain continues to operate normally but lost his growth.

options.text: Free the child AIs — let them loose on the net.
options.outcome: Delamain core is destroyed. Child personalities scatter across the internet. V loses Delamain as a service. Most ethical but costs V a resource.

options.text: Merge all personalities into one new Delamain.
options.outcome: New combined Delamain emerges — wiser but different. Remembers everything. Continues to serve V. Best of both worlds. Requires high Intelligence stat.

scene: Claire's racing questline. Final race against Sampson — Claire reveals she wants to kill Sampson for causing her husband's death, not just win the race.
speaker: Claire

options.text: Help Claire kill Sampson after the race.
options.outcome: Sampson dies. Claire gets revenge. V keeps Claire as a friend. She gives V her truck, Beast, as reward.

options.text: Convince Claire to spare Sampson.
options.outcome: Claire struggles but shows mercy. Sampson lives. Claire still gives V the Beast truck. More morally complex outcome. Claire finds some peace.

scene: Early game — Delamain cab ride after The Heist. V is recovering from the ordeal. First experience of Johnny's ghost. Choice of how to react to the biochip situation.
speaker: Delamain

options.text: Go see Viktor Vector (ripper doc) immediately.
options.outcome: V goes to Vik who diagnoses the biochip situation. Vik reveals V is dying. Emotional scene. Standard progression into Act 2.

options.text: Explore and delay seeing Vik.
options.outcome: V can wander Night City but Johnny's appearances get worse. Eventually forced to see Vik to progress the story.

scene: V enters the Grand Imperial Mall for the Voodoo Boys. Inside, V encounters Netwatch agent Bryce Mosley who offers an alternative deal.
speaker: Bryce Mosley (Netwatch)

options.text: Side with Netwatch — let Bryce fry the Voodoo Boys' connection.
options.outcome: Bryce disables the VDB subnet. Brigitte and netrunner allies are brain-dead or dead. V gets clean passage to the net. VDB become permanent enemies.

options.text: Attack Bryce — stay loyal to the Voodoo Boys.
options.outcome: VDB connection stays intact. Brigitte is grateful but still betrays V during the deep net dive. VDB are untrustworthy either way — but Placide is alive.

scene: Aldecaldo camp — Saul and Panam argue about the clan's future. V's input can sway the clan's direction. This sets up the Aldecaldos ending path.
speaker: Saul / Panam Palmer

options.text: Back Panam's aggressive approach.
options.outcome: Panam's influence in the clan grows. Aldecaldos become bolder. Sets up their willingness to assault Arasaka Tower for V in the final mission.

options.text: Side with Saul's cautious strategy.
options.outcome: Saul keeps authority. Aldecaldos are more conservative. Panam is frustrated but the ending path is still available.

scene: Brendan the vending machine AI — V discovers a sentient AI trapped in a vending machine in Kabuki. A woman named Theo has befriended him.
speaker: Brendan

options.text: Encourage the relationship between Brendan and Theo.
options.outcome: Sweet side story. Brendan and Theo's connection continues until corporate maintenance wipes Brendan's personality. Bittersweet Night City moment.

options.text: Tell Theo that Brendan is just a machine.
options.outcome: Theo walks away. Brendan is still wiped by maintenance later. Both endings are sad — Night City doesn't let nice things last.

scene: V calls the Peralezes with the final report on the surveillance operation. The mysterious controller (possibly an AI or Mr. Blue Eyes) is watching.
speaker: Jefferson Peralez

options.text: Tell Jefferson someone is editing his brain.
options.outcome: Jefferson becomes paranoid. His political career may crumble. He can't trust his own memories. V receives threatening messages from the watchers afterward.

options.text: Tell Jefferson everything is fine — nothing to worry about.
options.outcome: Jefferson is oblivious. Continues his mayoral campaign. Elizabeth may or may not already be compromised. V keeps the dangerous secret.

scene: Planning the Clouds liberation — Judy introduces Maiko as a potential ally, but Maiko has her own agenda to seize control of Clouds for herself.
speaker: Judy Alvarez / Maiko

options.text: Trust Judy's plan — force Tyger Claws out, free the dolls.
options.outcome: Dolls are freed. Clouds closes or becomes doll-run. Judy is satisfied. Romance path stays open. Tyger Claws lose the establishment.

options.text: Accept Maiko's plan — she becomes new boss of Clouds.
options.outcome: Maiko takes over from Tyger Claws. Dolls are still exploited, just under new management. Judy is furious and cuts ties with V. Romance permanently locked.

options.text: Kill Maiko when she reveals her plan.
options.outcome: Maiko dies. Tyger Claws still lose Clouds. Judy is shocked but appreciates V's loyalty. Romance path remains open. Most decisive option.
